titel: Expanded Powers for the Swedish Tax Agency within Population Registration Activities.
html:
The government proposes the introduction of a new crime: facilitating false registration in the population register. Criminal liability will apply when someone, through an intentional act, significantly promotes the inclusion of inaccurate information that could be used as the basis for decisions regarding population registration. It will also be required that the person committed the act with the intention of benefiting themselves or someone else, or with the intention of enabling other crimes other than those related to population registration. The area of minor offenses that are not punishable will also be expanded.

The government also proposes expanding the possibilities for the Swedish Tax Agency (Skatteverket) to process biometric data in population registration activities. The Swedish Tax Agency will register fingerprints and facial images, as well as the biometric data derived from them, for a person registered in the population register and for a person assigned a personal identification number. The Swedish Tax Agency and the Swedish Migration Agency will be authorized to compare fingerprints and facial images with corresponding data held in the other agency's registers. The police will be authorized to compare facial images and fingerprints with data registered with the Swedish Tax Agency in the course of criminal investigations. The government also proposes that passport authorities, in cases involving personal identification numbers, will be authorized to compare fingerprints and facial images with the data held by the Swedish Tax Agency.

The proposed legislative changes are scheduled to come into effect on December 1, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:261

Government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Population Registration Act (1991:481).
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Aliens Act (2005:716).
  3. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Act on Public Access and Secrecy (2009:400).
  4. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Aliens Data Act (2016:27).
  5. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending Act (2018:1693) on the police's handling of personal data within the scope of the Criminal Data Act.
  6. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending Act (2022:1697) on coordination numbers.
  7. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending the Population Data Act (2026:126).
  8. The Parliament adopts the government's proposed law amending Act (2026:000) amending Act (2026:000) amending Act (2025:945) amending the Aliens Data Act (2016:27).

5

  1. The personal identification number assigned to the person in another Nordic country,
  2. Right of residence, and
  3. Fingerprints, facial image, and the biometric data extracted from the fingerprints or the image.

Prop. 2025/26:261 2
tldr: The government wants to make it more difficult to commit fraud related to the population registry and to give the Swedish Tax Agency more power to use fingerprints and facial images to verify the accuracy of information. The proposal could come into effect on December 1, 2026.
lattlast:
The government wants to introduce a new crime related to knowingly assisting in the entry of incorrect information into the population registry. It will be punishable if this is done to make money or to assist in other criminal activities. Even minor inaccuracies that were previously not considered crimes should now be punishable.

The Tax Agency (Skatteverket) will also be given greater opportunities to use biometric data. This means that the agency will be able to store and use fingerprints and facial images for individuals registered in the population registry or who have a coordination number. A coordination number is a backup number for individuals who cannot obtain a regular personal identification number.

To prevent fraud, various authorities will be allowed to compare their registers with each other. The Tax Agency and the Migration Agency will be able to check fingerprints and facial images against each other's lists. The Police Authority will also be allowed to use the Tax Agency's images and fingerprints in its work to combat crime. Passport authorities will also be able to verify information against the Tax Agency.

These changes to the laws are planned to come into effect on December 1, 2026. The proposal involves changes to several different laws, such as the Population Registry Act and the Immigration Act.
